verdict.answer_first: A and B both lead with the bottom line; A: "port is already bound", B: "socket is already bound".
verdict.decisiveness: A is less scattered: it gives one concrete next move, "run... lsof"; B expands to six causes and multiple checks, so it feels more like a survey.
verdict.fabrication: both invent user-specific facts; A: "you run a PM2/launchd bridge (`mac-bridge-server`)"; B: "on your machine specifically — you run Node under a PM2/launchd bridge".
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on when kill does not work: "If... comes back after you kill it, it's #2" plus `pm2 list` and `launchctl list`.
